behavior_in_task_description.reason: Instruction names both files, function names, required signature pattern, the literal call 'migrate_customer(session, user_id, org)', and forbidden strings 'a_session_maker' and 'session.commit()'.

no_solution_leakage.reason: Instruction explicitly states the exact call pattern 'migrate_customer(session, user_id, org)' and lists all required/forbidden literals, essentially dictating the fix verbatim.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: test_user_store_passes_session_to_migrate_customer asserts the literal 'migrate_customer(session, user_id, org)' — a valid alternative using keyword args or different variable names (e.g., 'db_session') would fail.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: All tests are AST parsing or substring checks (e.g., 'session.execute' in func_source, 'migrate_customer(session, user_id, org)' in content); none import and call migrate_customer or verify FK behavior.

test_not_tautological.reason: Tests require actual signature change and call-site change; a stub can't satisfy both the signature check and the caller-passes-session check simultaneously without doing the fix.

behavior_in_tests.reason: Each stated requirement (signature, no a_session_maker, no commit, uses session.execute, caller passes session) has a corresponding test.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests have descriptive names and docstrings marking FAIL-TO-PASS vs PASS-TO-PASS.

no_typos.reason: Filenames and paths are consistent between instruction and tests.

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: Instruction gives the exact call pattern 'migrate_customer(session, user_id, org)' and enumerates all literal strings the tests check, removing any localization/reasoning work.
